goal: Reduce validate-plan test suite runtime by ~40% by removing nested regression checks and mocking gh CLI calls
architecture: Remove redundant nested test suite invocations from test_check_review.sh and test_review_gates.sh (CI already runs all files independently). Create a gh mock stub fixture that intercepts `gh pr list` calls and returns controlled responses via GH_MOCK_PR_COUNT env var. Update test_check_workflow.sh to use the mock and remove skip guards so PR-related tests run unconditionally.

phases.name: Remove nested regressions and mock gh CLI
phases.rationale: All three tasks modify disjoint files and can execute in parallel within a single phase.

tasks.name: Remove nested regression test invocations
tasks.done_when: test_check_review.sh has 11 tests (Test 12 removed), test_review_gates.sh has 11 tests (Test 11 removed, Tests 1-10 + 5b remain), both pass in under 2s each

tasks.name: Create gh mock stub fixture
tasks.verification: GH_MOCK_PR_COUNT=1 bash tests/validate-plan/fixtures/gh-mock.sh pr list --base main --head test --json number --jq 'length' && echo 'exit 0' || echo 'exit non-zero'
tasks.done_when: gh-mock.sh is executable, returns GH_MOCK_PR_COUNT value (default 0) on stdout for `pr list` commands, exits 0 for recognized commands, exits 1 for unrecognized commands

tasks.name: Wire gh mock into test_check_workflow.sh and remove skip guards
tasks.done_when: Tests 7 and 9 run unconditionally (no `command -v gh` guards), test uses PATH-prepended mock directory, all tests pass with zero network calls
